author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
title: The Sting of Death, What?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: But even in the passage to the Corinthians, where he had been treating fully of the resurrection, the apostle concludes his statement in such a way as not to permit us to doubt that the death of the body is the result of sin.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: For after he had said, “This corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality: so when this corruptible shall have put on incorruption, and this mortal immortality, then,” he added, “shall be brought to pass the saying which is written, Death is swallowed up in victory.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: O death, where is thy victory?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: O death, where is thy sting?” and at last he subjoined these words: “The sting of death is sin; and the strength of sin is the law.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: Now, because (as the apostle’s words most plainly declare) death shall then be swallowed up in victory when this corruptible and mortal shall have put on incorruption and immortality,—that is, when “God shall quicken even our mortal bodies by His Spirit that dwelleth in us,”—it manifestly follows that the sting of the body of this death, which is the contrary of the resurrection of the body, is sin.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: The sting, however, is that by which death was made, and not that which death made, since it is by sin that we die, and not by death that we sin.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: It is therefore called “the sting of death” on the principle which originated the phrase “the tree of life,”—not because the life of man produced it, but because by it the life of man was made.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: In like manner “the tree of knowledge” was that whereby man’s knowledge was made, not that which man made by his knowledge.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: So also “the sting of death” is that by which death was produced, not that which death made.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: We similarly use the expression “the cup of death,” since by it some one has died, or might die,—not meaning, of course, a cup made by a dying or dead man.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: The sting of death is therefore sin, because by the puncture of sin the human race has been slain.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: Why ask further: the death of what,—whether of the soul, or of the body?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: Whether the first which we are all of us now dying, or the second which the wicked hereafter shall die?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: There is no occasion for plying the question so curiously; there is no room for subterfuge.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: The words in which the apostle expresses the case answer the questions: “When this mortal,” says he, “shall have put on immortality, then shall be brought to pass the saying which is written, Death is swallowed up in victory.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: O death, where is thy victory?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: O death, where is thy sting?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: The sting of death is sin, and the strength of sin is the law.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: He was treating of the resurrection of the body, wherein death shall be swallowed up in victory, when this mortal shall have put on immortality.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: Then over death itself shall be raised the shout of triumph, when at the resurrection of the body it shall be swallowed up in victory; then shall be said to it, “O death, where is thy victory?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: O death, where is thy sting?”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: To the death of the body, therefore, is this said.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: For victorious immortality shall swallow it up, when this mortal shall put on immortality.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: I repeat it, to the death of the body shall it be said, “Where is thy victory?”—that victory in which thou didst conquer all, so that even the Son of God engaged in conflict with thee, and by not shrinking but grappling with thee overcame.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: In these that die thou hast conquered; but thou art thyself conquered in these that rise again.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: Thy victory was but temporal, in which thou didst swallow up the bodies of them that die.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: Our victory will abide eternal, in which thou art swallowed up in the bodies of them that rise again.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: “Where is thy sting?”—that is, the sin wherewithal we are punctured and poisoned, so that thou didst fix thyself in our very bodies, and for so long a time didst hold them in possession.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: “The sting of death is sin, and the strength of sin is the law.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: We all sinned in one, so that we all die in one; we received the law, not by amendment according to its precepts to put an end to sin, but by transgression to increase it.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: For “the law entered that sin might abound;” and “the Scripture hath concluded all under sin;” but “thanks be to God, who hath given us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ,” in order that “where sin abounded, grace might much more abound;” and “that the promise by faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe;” and that we might overcome death by a deathless resurrection, and sin, “the sting” thereof, by a free justification.
